summary: Large assembly data-splitting PR that removed about 5230 lines of label/data-table content from the monolithic asm/data.s and redistributed it into more specific asm translation-unit files through asm/melee/text_3.s. The split appended .section .data blocks to existing asm files such as asm/melee/db/code_80225374.s, asm/melee/gr/stage.s, many asm/melee/gr/grt*.s files, and asm/melee/text_3.s, while updating obj_files.mk to include new data-only asm objects for grtmars and grtness alongside their src objects. There was no PR body or review discussion in the available dump, so intent beyond the title and diff is inferred only from the file movement.
what_changed: Deleted the bulk of a large contiguous data block from asm/data.s, leaving only the include and trailing content visible in the diff excerpt.
Added approximately 2428 lines of .data content to asm/melee/text_3.s after existing text_3 labels.
Added approximately 869 lines of .data content to asm/melee/gr/stage.s.
Added approximately 509 lines of .data content to asm/melee/db/code_80225374.s.

Added per-module .data blocks to numerous gr target-test files named grt*.s, including grtclink, grtdonkey, grtdrmario, grtfalco, grtfalcon, grtfox, grticeclimber, grtmario, grtmars, grtness, and others.
Created/filled asm/melee/gr/grtmars.s and asm/melee/gr/grtness.s as data-only asm companions, each with .include "macros.inc" and .section .data.
Updated obj_files.mk to add $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/gr/grtmars.o and $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/gr/grtness.o before the existing src/melee/gr/grtmars.o and src/melee/gr/grtness.o entries.
smart_moves: Kept original lbl_803E**** and lbl_802**** symbol names intact while relocating data, minimizing risk of breaking existing assembly references.
Moved data into files that appear to correspond to the owning module or nearby text section, rather than keeping everything in the global asm/data.s bucket.
Preserved raw .4byte tables, NULL sentinels, function-pointer references, and encoded string constants during the split instead of attempting a risky semantic rewrite.
Added explicit .section .data markers in target files so relocated data remains section-correct.
Updated the object list when adding data-only asm companions for modules that already had src objects, which is necessary for the linker to see the split-out data.
decomp_lessons: For early-stage decomp cleanup, moving monolithic data.s content into the closest module-specific asm file can improve locality without requiring full C typing.
Data splits should preserve exact label order, alignment expectations, section selection, and raw word values until symbols and structs are better understood.
A module can need both a src object and an asm object when code has been partially decompiled but its data is still represented in assembly; obj_files.mk must include both in the intended order.
Large tables containing function pointers and file/path strings can be split by ownership first, then later converted to typed C arrays once their structure is known.
When moving data referenced by text labels, keep label names stable so future matching and cross-reference work remains searchable.
naming_conventions: Unknown data labels retained address-derived names such as lbl_803E8548, lbl_803E8598, lbl_803EA6C8, and lbl_803EAE68.
Unknown code labels and functions retained address-derived names such as lbl_8021F9CC and func_8021F840.
Some already-named module functions were preserved in tables, for example GrTMars_80222080 and GrTNess_8022275C.
Target-test ground files use the grt character/module naming pattern, including grtmario, grtclink, grtdonkey, grtmars, grtness, grtpurin, grtseak, and grtzelda.
Assembly companion files mirror module names under asm/melee/gr/, while decompiled code may remain under src/melee/gr/ with the same basename.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use .include "macros.inc" at the top of newly added asm data files.
Place relocated objects under .section .data, and return to .bss or .sbss only where the surrounding file requires it.
Represent unknown tables and strings with .4byte entries, including NULL, 0xC0000000 sentinels, encoded ASCII path fragments such as /GrT*.dat, and diagnostic format strings.
Maintain original global labels with .global directives after relocation so linker references remain valid.
When splitting data into a file with existing BSS/SBSS, insert the .data block before the subsequent section directive shown in the target file.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review bodies were present in the provided PR dump.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that typed or renamed the grt*.s data tables moved out of asm/data.s in PR 196.
Search for subsequent changes to asm/melee/gr/grtmars.s and asm/melee/gr/grtness.s to see when their data-only asm companions were removed or converted.
Inspect later obj_files.mk history for patterns involving both asm/melee/gr/*.o and src/melee/gr/*.o entries for the same module.
Look for PRs that continue the data.s split beyond text_3 or finish removing the monolithic asm/data.s bucket.
